title: Decadent Greek Potato Pastitsio
description: A jammy, glassy-pastitsio that showcases russet potatoes and smoked salmon in scallop shells.
instructions: Peel and slice 600g russet potatoes into 5mm thick rounds; set aside.
In a saucepan, heat 2 tbsp olive oil over medium heat; sauté diced onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add 400g canned diced tomatoes and 1 tsp honey to the pan; simmer until the mixture thickens to a jammy consistency, about 10 minutes.
Stir in 200g smoked salmon, crumbled into small pieces, and season with salt and pepper; cook for 2 minutes.
In a separate pot, melt 30g butter and whisk in 20g flour to make a roux; gradually add 200ml milk, stirring until thick béchamel forms.
Combine the tomato-salmon mixture with the béchamel, adding 60g crumbled feta; whisk until smooth.
Lightly grease a baking dish lined with scallop shells; layer the dish with potato rounds, then pour the sauce mixture over, covering the shells completely.
Sprinkle 25g breadcrumbs over the top and bake for 35 minutes until the edges are golden and the sauce is glossy.
Let the dish rest 5 minutes before serving, then garnish with additional smoked salmon slices or fresh dill if desired.
